title: Download audit trail of pulses including sub items
excerpt: I am trying to download all of the updates, emails and files on a pulse so I can upload it to our “back office system” to log as an audit trail. This is important because when we submit some business (We are financial ad…

posts.author: davidabel (Mr David Abel)
posts.cookedHtml:
I am trying to download all of the updates, emails and files on a pulse so I can upload it to our “back office system” to log as an audit trail. This is important because when we submit some business (We are financial advisors so this could be advising on a pension or investment), we need to record all of the communications with a client and providers to ensure that, if there is ever a complaint, we can have all the information saved in one place.

Is there anyway of doing this? I know there is an audit trail button but it doesn’t include the sub items updates and files.

Thanks in advance.

posts.author: CharlotteK (Charlotte Kirkham)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey there!

It sounds like you will want to add the Files tab to your Updates Section (to see all the files on that item) and then use the Updates section export to Excel button to get all the updates and emails (if you have Emails & Activities on your Updates section):

Screen Shot 2024-01-26 at 6.51.19 PM1226×598 39.5 KB

Then, you will go to the Files tab and click “download all as a zip” to get all the files from that item:

Screen Shot 2024-01-26 at 6.53.18 PM1250×428 33.6 KB

While it is not ideal that this is a two-step process, hopefully it helps you get what you need!

posts.author: davidabel (Mr David Abel)
posts.cookedHtml:
Good afternoon Charlotte, thanks for the response.

This doesn’t work as it doesn’t copy any updates on the subitems. See photo.
As we have 15 subitems per pulse, this would mean exporting each subitem to excel. IS there a way that this can be done all at once?

image1660×531 122 KB

posts.author: Iryna_Belaya (Iryna Belaya)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @davidabel ,

If it’s still relevant to you, Board Email Report can be a workaround for your case. There are 2 types of automated reports on a board and item level. On an item level you can get the following reports:

  • Updates by a specific employee

  • Updates mentioning a specific employee

  • Updates containing attachments

  • Status updates

  • Last updates

You set up automation rules to get reports every day (week, month) with consolidated updates and detailed xls-report as an attachment. This is how the result looks:

image424×469 24.9 KB

image1462×439 83.1 KB

You can test the app and check if it works for you. I hope you’ll find it helpful. Also, our team can make custom automation for your case in terms of item updates, just reach out to us.
